<!-- loading: on-demand (invoked via the Skill tool when needed) — generated by scripts/list-skills.sh --annotate; do not hand-edit -->

# CI conventions — the merge gate (PR L)

The pipeline's gates run pre-merge on the author's machine; **CI re-RUNS every objective
check on the merge commit** as the source of truth. It never re-reads `.pipeline/`
artifacts — those are the author's claims; re-execution is the only honest verification.
Full rationale: `docs/ci-merge-gate-plan.md`.

## What CI does and does not re-verify (the honest-scope table)

| Property | CI mechanism |
|---|---|
| Tests + coverage floor | re-run natively (same floor figure as the local gate) |
| SAST / SCA / secrets / IaC | re-run Semgrep, OSV, Gitleaks, Trivy fs, Checkov |
| ASVS Tier-1 patterns / source markers / lockfile integrity | re-run the `scripts/ci/` copies with `SCAN_BASE` |
| Store compliance (mobile targets) | re-run `store-compliance.sh` (repo-state scoped; no-ops elsewhere) |
| Criteria coverage (`criteria_covered`) | **NOT re-derivable** — agent judgment; guarded by required human review + the `docs/decisions/` record in the diff |
| Human checkpoints (plan/diff/design-approved) | **no CI analog** — branch protection + required review is the counterpart |
| ASVS 6g / DP / input-surface reconciliations | **NOT re-runnable** — pre-merge guarantees; the pattern-checkable subset rides the re-run scanners |

Never claim CI re-verifies the last three rows. Green CI = "objective checks hold on the
merge commit," not "all agent judgments re-confirmed."

## The re-run contract (`scripts/ci/` + `SCAN_BASE`)

- Bootstrap copies `asvs-sast.sh`, `guard-source-markers.sh`, `lockfile-check.sh`,
  `store-compliance.sh`, `dast-review.sh` into the project's `scripts/ci/` — committed and
  human-reviewed, so CI never fetches check logic remotely. They're pinned at the
  bootstrap-time version; refresh deliberately (re-copy + review the diff), never silently.
- `SCAN_BASE=<merge-base ref>` rescopes the diff-scoped scripts to `git diff SCAN_BASE...HEAD`
  (on a merge commit `git diff HEAD` is empty — a naive re-run passes vacuously). In this
  mode they skip the `.pipeline/state.json` guard, scan committed files only, **fail closed
  on an unresolvable ref**, and signal by exit code (2 = fail the job).
- Checkout with `fetch-depth: 0` in any job that uses `SCAN_BASE` (it needs history).

## The CI waiver channel

CI honors **only committed, tool-native ignore files**: `osv-scanner.toml`, `.trivyignore`,
`.semgrepignore` — visible in the diff, therefore human-reviewed at the diff checkpoint.
The local `.pipeline/waivers.json` is gitignored and never reaches CI **by design**; a
local-only waiver that never lands in a committed ignore file will (correctly) fail the CI
re-run. `asvs-sast` and `guard-source-markers` are **fix-not-waive** — no ignore path exists.

## Branch-protection checklist (operator, once per repo)

Configuration, not a hook — this checklist is the auditable record. After the first CI run
(contexts must exist before they can be required):

```bash
gh api -X PUT "repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/main/protection" --input - <<'JSON'
{
  "required_status_checks": { "strict": true, "contexts": [
    "build-and-test", "sast", "deps", "secrets", "containers-iac",
    "asvs-markers", "store-compliance" ] },
  "required_pull_request_reviews": { "dismiss_stale_reviews": true },
  "enforce_admins": true,
  "allow_force_pushes": false,
  "allow_deletions": false,
  "restrictions": null
}
JSON
```

For the engine repo itself, the single required context is `eval` (Layer 1's workflow).

## Workflow conventions (baked into the template)

First-party actions pinned by commit SHA; scanners run from official images (pin digests on
first run); `permissions: contents: read` default with per-job elevation; per-ref
`concurrency`; hard `timeout-minutes`; **no long-lived cloud keys** — OIDC only, per
`iac-conventions`.

## How delivery chains on (PR M/N)

Deploy workflows trigger via `workflow_run` on this workflow's success on `main` —
**`build-provenance.yml`** (PR M: build → sign → SBOM/SLSA-attest → push; see
`delivery-conventions`), then staging → progressive prod delivery (PR N,
`docs/environments-delivery-plan.md`). The reserved `dast-baseline` job slot is filled per
`docs/dast-plan.md`; CodeQL (row CQ) lands as one additional job when adopted.
